pr.title: Revit 2025 + 2026 build targets (net8.0-windows) + deploy scripts — lane-3A operator target is 2025
pr.description:
## Summary

De-risks Lane-3A Gate 2: the operator no longer improvises MSBuild overrides — Gate 2 is now `.\scripts\deploy-revit-2025.ps1 -BuildOnly` then deploy. **Revit 2025 is the lane-3A operator target** (machine has 2024/2025/2027 installed; 2026 not yet). The 2026 target is included and kept build-ready for when 2026 is installed.

- New per-version build targets mirroring the 2027 SDK-style pattern but on **net8.0-windows** (Revit 2025/2026 add-ins run .NET 8): `Axiom.Revit.2025.sln` / `Axiom.Revit.2026.sln`, `Axiom.Core.<ver>` + `Axiom.RevitAddin.<ver>` projects compiling the shared `Axiom.Core` / `Axiom.RevitAddin` sources via `Compile Include` links with `REVIT_2025` / `REVIT_2026` symbols and Revit API HintPaths under `$(ProgramW6432)\Autodesk\Revit <ver>\`. No capability logic duplicated.
- `RevitElementIdCompat`: `.Value` now gated on `#if REVIT_2025 || REVIT_2026 || REVIT_2027` (2024 baseline byte-identical — no `REVIT_2025/2026` symbol in the net48 solution).
- `scripts/deploy-revit-2025.ps1` / `deploy-revit-2026.ps1` (`-BuildOnly` supported; .NET SDK ≥ 8 check; deploys manifest + `Axiom.RevitAddin.dll` + `Axiom.Core.dll` + `Newtonsoft.Json.dll` to `C:\ProgramData\Autodesk\Revit\Addins\<ver>\` — the 2024 all-users pattern; Program Files remains the 2027 deviation, untouched).
- Lane-3A runbook: operator target section rewritten to Revit 2025 (2026 when installed); Gate 2, prerequisites, operator fields and evidence-to-return now reference the 2025 script/paths. Multi-version runbook + `revit-adapter` SKILL updated in the same PR.

**Caveat:** both targets are **not build-verified** — no Revit 2025/2026 DLLs on this box. The first `dotnet build` happens on the operator machine as lane-3A Gate 2 itself; the runbook says record any deviations as findings, and a build failure is a lane-3A finding, not a silent fallback to another version.

## Tests

XML/sln well-formedness verified locally; no Python changes → no pytest (tier 3: C# build validation happens on the operator machine).

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
